page: UpCloud PREMIUM-32xCPU-64GB is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor. It features 32 physical cores and 32 vCPUs with a shared CPU allocation, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 400 GB of local SSD storage. No GPU accelerators are present on this instance.

Benchmark data shows top-tier single-core CPU performance, with stress-ng single-core and bzip3 single-threaded decompression scoring in the top 10% of tested servers. Multi-core performance is also strong, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU score, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory read bandwidth is strong at 230.25 GB/sec for large blocks, though memory write bandwidth falls into the average tier. Notably, the PassMark memory score is weak, placing in the bottom 25%, indicating a potential bottleneck for memory-intensive operations despite the high core count.

Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and strong compute benchmarks, this server is positioned for compute-heavy workloads. It performs well in database operations, with PassMark database tests and Redis SET operations scoring in the top 25%. The inclusion of AVX-512 and cryptography instruction sets supports vector and encryption tasks. However, the shared CPU allocation and lower memory-to-core ratio make it less suitable for memory-bound applications. It represents a resource-dense option for batch processing, web serving, and database hosting where raw compute density is prioritized over dedicated CPU cores.
description: The UpCloud PREMIUM-32xCPU-64GB is an x86_64 server featuring 32 vCPUs and 32 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM (2.0 GB per core) and 400 GB of local SSD storage, with no GPU hardware. Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU capabilities, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core and bzip3 decompression tests. Multi-core performance, database operations, and Redis SET throughput rank in the top 25%. However, the server exhibits a tradeoff with a weak PassMark memory score in the bottom 25% and utilizes a shared CPU allocation. This configuration is applicable for compute-intensive tasks, database operations, and web serving that benefit from high core counts and vector instruction sets but do not require dedicated CPU resources or massive memory-per-core ratios.
og_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-32xCPU-64GB is an x86_64 server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 400 GB SSD. It features top-tier single-core performance but exhibits a weak PassMark memory score.
meta_description: UpCloud PREMIUM-32xCPU-64GB is an x86_64 server with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 400 GB SSD, configured for compute-intensive workloads.
tagline: A compute-focused x86_64 server featuring thirty-two AMD EPYC cores, local SSD storage, and top-tier single-core processing performance.
